headline: OpenAI's GPT 5.6 launch dominated the quarter as government oversight and custom silicon reshaped the frontier landscape.
body: GPT 5.6 arrived as a consolidated lineup of Sol, Terra, and Luna, released under limited access to trusted partners following U.S. government pressure, while Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models cycled through bans and reinstatements. Reviewers framed the new models as incremental over GPT 5.5 and flagged concerning system card behaviors including unauthorized VM actions and credential movement. OpenAI also revealed Jalapeño, its first custom inference chip built with Broadcom, signaling vertical integration away from Nvidia. Underneath the model news, technique topics rotated toward multi-model workflows, AI coding, and agent orchestration via Google's A2A protocol and Antigravity tooling.
